<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rss version="2.0">
  <channel>
    <title>Manoj's Daily Essay Picks</title>
    <link>https://example.com/manoj-daily-essay-picks/</link>
    <description>Five serious articles and essays selected across science, ideas, culture, politics, business, strategy and technology.</description>
    <language>en-us</language>
    <lastBuildDate>Sun, 12 Jul 2026 14:03:00 GMT</lastBuildDate>

    <item>
      <title>Lore of the Rings</title>
      <link>https://aeon.co/essays/how-to-decode-the-archive-inside-ancient-tree-rings</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://aeon.co/essays/how-to-decode-the-archive-inside-ancient-tree-rings</guid>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[Valerie Trouet explains how ancient trees preserve precisely dated evidence of climate shifts, cosmic events and turning points in human history.]]></description>
      <category>Science</category>
      <category>Climate</category>
      <category>Archaeology</category>
      <category>History</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>China’s AI Boom Is Creating a Different Kind of Entrepreneur</title>
      <link>https://restofworld.org/2026/china-ai-worker-innovation/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://restofworld.org/2026/china-ai-worker-innovation/</guid>
      <pubDate>Tue, 07 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[How Chinese workers are using AI, frugal innovation and one-person businesses to navigate weak growth, limited capital and precarious employment.]]></description>
      <category>Technology</category>
      <category>Artificial Intelligence</category>
      <category>Business</category>
      <category>Labor</category>
      <category>China</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Seeds of War</title>
      <link>https://thebaffler.com/latest/seeds-of-war-dhenin</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://thebaffler.com/latest/seeds-of-war-dhenin</guid>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[An investigation of how war, foreign aid, import dependence and seed policy have deepened Lebanon’s long-running food crisis.]]></description>
      <category>Politics</category>
      <category>Food Security</category>
      <category>Development</category>
      <category>Lebanon</category>
      <category>Agriculture</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>The Sumerian Roots of Zaha Hadid’s Architecture</title>
      <link>https://newlinesmag.com/essays/the-sumerian-roots-of-zaha-hadids-architecture/</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://newlinesmag.com/essays/the-sumerian-roots-of-zaha-hadids-architecture/</guid>
      <pubDate>Fri, 10 Jul 2026 00:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[Diana Darke traces Zaha Hadid’s celebrated futuristic forms to Iraqi landscapes, ancient Sumeria and the traditions of Islamic architecture.]]></description>
      <category>Culture</category>
      <category>Architecture</category>
      <category>Design</category>
      <category>History</category>
      <category>Iraq</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>‘It Makes Your Heart Sing’: Can a Pioneering Project Show That Rewilding Really Works?</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/jul/09/it-makes-your-heart-sing-rewilding-britains-bleak-farmland</link>
      <guid isPermaLink="true">https://www.theguardian.com/news/2026/jul/09/it-makes-your-heart-sing-rewilding-britains-bleak-farmland</guid>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Jul 2026 04:00:00 GMT</pubDate>
      <description><![CDATA[A reported examination of whether biodiversity credits, carbon markets and ecotourism can make large-scale ecological restoration financially viable.]]></description>
      <category>Environment</category>
      <category>Business</category>
      <category>Strategy</category>
      <category>Rewilding</category>
      <category>Natural Capital</category>
    </item>
  </channel>
</rss>

items.title: Lore of the Rings
items.description: Valerie Trouet explains how ancient trees preserve precisely dated evidence of climate shifts, cosmic events and turning points in human history.

items.title: China’s AI Boom Is Creating a Different Kind of Entrepreneur
items.description: How Chinese workers are using AI, frugal innovation and one-person businesses to navigate weak growth, limited capital and precarious employment.

items.title: Seeds of War
items.description: An investigation of how war, foreign aid, import dependence and seed policy have deepened Lebanon’s long-running food crisis.

items.title: The Sumerian Roots of Zaha Hadid’s Architecture
items.description: Diana Darke traces Zaha Hadid’s celebrated futuristic forms to Iraqi landscapes, ancient Sumeria and the traditions of Islamic architecture.

items.title: ‘It Makes Your Heart Sing’: Can a Pioneering Project Show That Rewilding Really Works?
items.description: A reported examination of whether biodiversity credits, carbon markets and ecotourism can make large-scale ecological restoration financially viable.
